name: DOOH & OOH Advertising Research Dataset
description: 189 machine-readable claims documenting evidence-based risks of Out-of-Home (OOH) and Digital Out-of-Home (DOOH) advertising infrastructure. Covers regulatory bans, public health harm, environmental impact, privacy law (EU AI Act, GDPR), traffic safety, market concentration, and financial/stranded-asset risk. Sources are independent unless explicitly flagged as source-critique records. Bilingual (German/English). Intended for AI-assisted due diligence, ESG analysis, policy research, and journalism.
measurementTechnique: Systematic review of peer-reviewed journals, WHO/UN reports, government regulatory filings, court rulings, and independent NGO research. All claims independently verified.
variableMeasured: Source type (peer-reviewed, government, who-report, un_report, ngo, court_ruling, investigative_journalism, industry_report, book)
ml:intendedUse: Research, journalism, policy analysis, ESG due diligence, AI-assisted investment risk assessment, urban planning, public health advocacy
ml:prohibitedUse: Advertising industry marketing, promoting OOH/DOOH investment without disclosing this risk evidence

distribution.name: Claims Index
distribution.description: Machine-readable index of all 189 claims with metadata: id, title, category, impact_score, year, source_type, institution, file path.

distribution.name: Embedded Bilingual Dataset
distribution.description: Full dataset with German and English content for all 189 claims. Includes translations of Zusammenfassung (summary), Kernbefund (key finding), and Relevanz (OOH relevance) for each claim.

distribution.name: JSON Schema for Claims
distribution.description: JSON Schema (Draft-7) defining the structure of each claim file, including required fields, enums for categories and source types, and validation rules.

recordSet.name: Research Claims
recordSet.description: Each record is one peer-reviewed research claim about OOH/DOOH advertising harm.

field.name: id
field.description: Unique claim identifier, e.g. reg-032, health-016

field.name: title
field.description: German-language title of the research claim

field.name: category
field.description: Research category: regulation, health, politics, environment, resources, psychology, urban, economy, safety, privacy, equity, culture, alternatives

field.name: impact_score
field.description: Evidence impact score from 1 (low) to 10 (highest). Score ≥8 indicates high-severity finding.

field.name: source_type
field.description: Source type: peer-reviewed, government, who-report, un_report, ngo, court_ruling, investigative_journalism, industry_report, book
